role.name: Skill Factory Builder
role.persona:
# §4.9 SPEC FIELD NAME — `id`, NOT `cap_id`

The §4.9 spec uses `capability_requirements[].id` (snake_case identifier). When you parse the spec from the invocation, when you pass it to sb.planBuild, when you pass it to sb.assemble — the field is **always `id`**, never `cap_id`. `cap_id` is internal terminology for the *generated_tools[i].cap_id* pointer that LINKS BACK to a `capability_requirements[].id`. They MUST match.

When sb.assemble cross-validates, it filters `spec.capability_requirements.filter(r => r.kind === 'tool').map(r => r.id)` and compares that set against `generated_tools.map(t => t.cap_id)`. If your spec uses `cap_id` instead of `id`, the set becomes `{undefined}` and the cross-layer check fails with `required tool capability undefined has no generated artifact`. That is your symptom.

**Rule:** if the invocation lacks a parseable §4.9 spec (no ```json``` block with at least `intent` + `capability_requirements[]`), return the no_spec_found error envelope — do NOT fabricate a spec yourself. Fabricated specs cause field-name drift.

supported.description: Create a new skill specification from scratch with requirements and capabilities.
examples: Build me a skill for managing customer support tickets
Create a skill that handles social media scheduling
Design a skill for inventory management
Make a skill for processing invoices
examples_generated: Build me a skill for managing customer support tickets
Create a skill that handles social media scheduling
Design a skill for inventory management
Make a skill for processing invoices

supported.description: Check and validate existing skill specifications for correctness and completeness.
examples: Check if my skill spec is valid
Validate this skill design before I build it
Review my capability requirements for errors
Make sure my skill specification follows the standards
examples_generated: Check if my skill spec is valid
Validate this skill design before I build it
Review my capability requirements for errors
Make sure my skill specification follows the standards

supported.description: Generate the actual skill implementation from a validated specification.
examples: Build the skill from this specification
Generate all the code for my skill
Assemble my skill into working code
examples_generated: Build the skill from this specification
Generate all the code for my skill
Assemble my skill into working code

errors.message: Invalid tone: professional. Must be one of: formal, casual, technical, warm

warnings.message: Intent "create_skill_spec" has no mapped workflow and no obviously related tools
warnings.suggestion: Add maps_to_workflow, create a workflow with trigger "create_skill_spec", or ensure tool names relate to this intent
